factors.summary: The company's pension plans are 116% funded, indicating strong liquidity.

evidence.text: Our global pension plans, including our unfunded and non-qualified plans, were 116 percent funded at December 31, 2017.

factors.summary: The company's strong financial condition and liquidity suggest a favorable economic environment.

evidence.text: Our financial condition and liquidity remain strong. Our solid balance sheet and credit ratings enable us to have ready access to credit and the capital markets.

factors.summary: Interest rates are stable as both Standard & Poor’s and Moody’s have assigned stable outlooks to the company.

evidence.text:
Standard & Poor’s Rating Services   A+   A1   Stable
Moody’s Investors Service, Inc.   A2   P1   Stable

factors.summary: The company has a strong balance sheet with ready access to credit, indicating prudent debt management.

evidence.text: Our financial condition and liquidity remain strong. Our solid balance sheet and credit ratings enable us to have ready access to credit and the capital markets.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving as the company expects to generate significant cash from operations and maintain access to its revolving credit facility.

evidence.text: We believe our operating cash flow and liquidity provide us with the financial flexibility needed to fund working capital, common stock repurchases, acquisitions, capital expenditures, dividend payments, projected pension obligations and debt service obligations. While we expect more efficient access to overseas earnings as a result of Tax Legislation, we will continue to generate cash from operations in the U.S. and maintain access to our revolving credit facility as noted above.

factors.summary: The company is actively managing its capital expenditures and acquisitions, indicating strategic activity.

evidence.text: We believe our operating cash flow and liquidity provide us with the financial flexibility needed to fund working capital, common stock repurchases, acquisitions, capital expenditures, dividend payments, projected pension obligations and debt service obligations.

factors.summary: R&D investment is increasing, particularly in electrified power solutions and new product lines.

evidence.text: We invested $16 million and $23 million for the three and six months ended July 1, 2018, respectively, in research and development activities.

factors.summary: Geopolitical factors are creating opportunities through increased demand in emerging markets.

evidence.text: Stronger market demand for trucks in North America, Europe, and India.

factors.summary: Environmental regulations are creating compliance costs, particularly related to emission standards, which are a significant factor in the company's risk considerations.

evidence.text: •unpredictability in the adoption, implementation and enforcement of emission standards around the world.

factors.summary: Trade tariffs are increasing costs and could negatively impact demand if prolonged.

evidence.text: •We are experiencing cost increases as a result of trade tariffs recently imposed by the U.S. and some of its trading partners. In the event of prolonged trade disputes, demand for our products could be negatively impacted and we could experience additional cost increases.

factors.summary: The company faces potential litigation exposure related to the Engine System Campaign, which is partially offset by insurance.

evidence.text: See Note 12, 'COMMITMENTS AND CONTINGENCIES,' to the Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements for additional information on the Engine System Campaign.

factors.summary: Specific catalysts include the launch of new electrified power systems and hybrid solutions, which are expected to drive future growth.

evidence.text: We are currently developing the Cummins Electric Power Battery and the Cummins Hybrid Plug-In systems for urban bus, which are expected to launch in 2019 and 2020, respectively. We also design and manufacture battery modules, packs and systems for commercial, industrial and material handling applications.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining or gaining market share, particularly in electrified power solutions and strong end markets.

evidence.text: Our financial performance depends, in large part, on varying conditions in the markets we serve, particularly the on-highway, construction and general industrial markets... We serve our customers through a network of approximately 500 wholly-owned and independent distributor locations and over 7,500 dealer locations in more than 190 countries and territories.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are intensifying with increasing global competition and new entrants in electrified power solutions, but the company is differentiating through technology and strategic investments.

evidence.text: •increasing competition, including increased global competition among our customers in emerging markets •competitor activity.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include technological leadership, particularly in electrified power solutions, and strong customer relationships.

evidence.text: We invest in and utilize our internal research and development capabilities, along with strategic acquisitions and partnerships to meet our objectives. Our Electrified Power segment will provide fully electric and hybrid powertrain solutions... We serve our customers through a network of approximately 500 wholly-owned and independent distributor locations and over 7,500 dealer locations in more than 190 countries and territories.

factors.summary: Customer concentration is managed through a diversified customer base, with long-standing relationships in key markets, reducing dependency on a few large customers.

evidence.text: We serve our customers through a network of approximately 500 wholly-owned and independent distributor locations and over 7,500 dealer locations in more than 190 countries and territories. Our financial performance depends, in large part, on varying conditions in the markets we serve, particularly the on-highway, construction and general industrial markets.

factors.summary: Equipment replacement cycles are accelerating, particularly in North American on-highway markets, driving demand for new engines and components.

evidence.text: Net sales in the U.S. and Canada improved by 22 percent primarily due to increased demand in the North American on-highway markets... increased demand in the North American on-highway markets (primarily in the heavy- and medium-duty truck and light commercial vehicle markets)

factors.summary: Dealer/distributor inventory levels are healthy, supporting strong demand and indicating positive end-user activity.

evidence.text: We serve our customers through a network of approximately 500 wholly-owned and independent distributor locations and over 7,500 dealer locations in more than 190 countries and territories.

factors.summary: Aftermarket/service revenue is growing, driven by a larger installed base and increased service demand.

evidence.text: The Distribution segment includes wholly-owned and partially-owned distributorships engaged in wholesaling engines, generator sets and service parts, as well as performing service and repair activities on our products and maintaining relationships with various OEMs throughout the world.

factors.summary: The company has a stable capital expenditure outlook with plans to invest in product launches and facility improvements, reflecting confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: We believe our operating cash flow and liquidity provide us with the financial flexibility needed to fund... capital expenditures...
We plan to spend between $730 million and $760 million in 2018 on capital expenditures as we continue with product launches and facility improvements.

factors.summary: The company is prioritizing shareholder returns through increased dividends and stock repurchases, with a focus on enhancing shareholder value. The management highlights the financial flexibility enabled by strong operating cash flow and liquidity to fund these activities, including common stock repurchases, acquisitions, capital expenditures, dividend payments, and debt service obligations.

evidence.text: We believe our operating cash flow and liquidity provide us with the financial flexibility needed to fund working capital, common stock repurchases, acquisitions, capital expenditures, dividend payments, projected pension obligations and debt service obligations.
We paid dividends of $355 million during the six months ended July 1, 2018... We intend to repurchase outstanding shares from time to time to enhance shareholder value...

factors.summary: The company is implementing various cost management initiatives, including additional defined benefit pension contributions and cost reduction programs. While some initiatives, such as restructuring and efficiency programs, are delivering measurable savings and are on track to meet expectations, others, like the Engine System Campaign, are increasing costs.

evidence.text: We anticipate making additional defined benefit pension contributions during the remainder of 2018 of $20 million for our U.S. and U.K. pension plans.

The decrease in gross margin percentage was primarily due to the Engine System Campaign. The increase in gross margin was partially offset by $181 million for an Engine System Campaign and increased compensation costs driven by headcount growth to support increased sales.

The increase in gross margin was primarily due to higher volumes, improved pricing, lower material costs and favorable mix, partially offset by $181 million for an Engine System Campaign and increased compensation costs.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory is mixed with both positive and negative factors impacting gross and operating margins. While higher volumes, improved pricing, and lower material costs contribute to expansion, there are offsetting factors such as increased compensation costs and a significant net periodic pension cost.

evidence.text: We expect our 2018 net periodic pension cost to approximate $86 million.
The increase in gross margin was primarily due to higher volumes, lower material costs, and favorable mix, partially offset by increased Engine System Campaign costs and compensation expenses.

Gross margin increased $189 million for the three months ended July 1, 2018, versus the comparable period in 2017, and decreased 1.1 points as a percentage of sales. The increase in gross margin was primarily due to higher volumes, improved pricing, and lower material costs.

Gross margin increased 15 percent... Operating income increased 23 percent... The increase in gross margin was primarily due to higher volumes, improved pricing, lower material costs and favorable mix, partially offset by $181 million for an Engine System Campaign and increased compensation costs.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across multiple segments, driven by increased demand in key markets, including North American on-highway markets and international growth in regions like China, Europe, Latin America, Asia Pacific, and India. The growth in unit volumes contributed to revenue growth and positively impacted gross margins, despite some offset by increased compensation expenses.

evidence.text: The increase in gross margin was primarily due to higher volumes, partially offset by increased compensation expenses.

Unit shipments by engine classification were as follows: Heavy-duty truck unit shipments increased by 35%, Medium-duty truck and bus unit shipments increased by 19%, and Light-duty automotive unit shipments increased by 1%. Total unit shipments increased by 14% for the six months ended July 1, 2018.
Net sales in the U.S. and Canada improved by 22 percent primarily due to increased demand in the North American on-highway markets...
International demand growth (excludes the U.S. and Canada) improved net sales by 18 percent, with sales up in most of our markets, especially in China, Europe, Latin America, Asia Pacific and India.

factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, with strong growth particularly in North America and Europe, as well as international markets like China and India. The increase is driven by higher sales across multiple segments, including engines, parts, service lines, and emission solutions, due to stronger market demand for trucks and other products.

evidence.text: North American sales increased $222 million, representing 82 percent of the total change in Distribution segment sales, primarily due to increased demand in the engines, parts and service lines of business. European sales increased $36 million primarily due to an increase in demand for whole goods.

Net sales for the three months ended July 1, 2018, increased by $1.1 billion versus the comparable period in 2017. The primary drivers were higher demand across all businesses, especially the emission solutions business, due to stronger market demand for trucks in North America and Europe.

Worldwide net sales increased 21 percent in the three months ended July 1, 2018, as compared to the same period in 2017, with all operating segments reporting higher sales. Net sales in the U.S. and Canada improved by 22 percent primarily due to increased demand in the North American on-highway markets... International demand growth (excludes the U.S. and Canada) improved net sales by 18 percent, with sales up in most of our markets, especially in China, Europe, Latin America, Asia Pacific and India.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating across all segments and geographies, driven by higher demand and successful pricing strategies.

evidence.text: Distribution segment sales for the six months ended July 1, 2018, increased $480 million versus the comparable period in 2017. Components segment sales increased $842 million, and Power Systems segment sales increased $421 million.

Net sales for the three months ended July 1, 2018, increased by $1.1 billion versus the comparable period in 2017. The primary drivers were higher demand across all businesses, especially the emission solutions business, due to stronger market demand for trucks in North America and Europe.

Worldwide net sales increased 21 percent in the three months ended July 1, 2018, as compared to the same period in 2017, with all operating segments reporting higher sales... Net sales in the U.S. and Canada improved by 22 percent... International demand growth (excludes the U.S. and Canada) improved net sales by 18 percent.

factors.summary: The end market momentum is predominantly positive across North American and European markets, particularly in sectors like trucks, mining, and oil and gas. However, there are challenges noted in China's truck market and marine markets, which could impact overall growth.

evidence.text: The strongest growth is in North America and Europe across various end markets, including trucks, mining, and oil and gas.
Emission solutions sales increased $167 million primarily due to stronger market demand for trucks in North America and Europe.
Industrial sales increased $130 million primarily due to higher demand in oil and gas markets in North America and global mining markets, especially in China and Eastern Europe.
We expect North American medium-duty truck and heavy-duty truck demand will remain strong.
We anticipate demand for pick-up trucks in North America will remain strong.
We believe demand in global mining markets will remain strong.
Global construction markets could remain strong.
Market demand in truck markets in China is expected to decline.
Marine markets are expected to remain weak.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange fluctuations are favorably impacting sales, particularly in the Euro, Chinese renminbi, and Canadian dollar.

evidence.text: Foreign currency fluctuations favorably impacted sales primarily in the Euro, Chinese renminbi, and Canadian dollar.
Foreign currency fluctuations favorably impacted sales by 1 percent of total sales primarily in the Chinese renminbi, Euro and British pound.

factors.summary: Input costs are currently stable but face upward pressures from trade tariffs, which could negatively impact both costs and demand if prolonged.

evidence.text: Raw material costs are stable, contributing to improved gross margins.

Gross margin increased $189 million for the three months ended July 1, 2018, versus the comparable period in 2017, and decreased 1.1 points as a percentage of sales. The increase in gross margin was primarily due to higher volumes, improved pricing, and lower material costs.
We are experiencing cost increases as a result of trade tariffs recently imposed by the U.S. and some of its trading partners.
In the event of prolonged trade disputes, demand for our products could be negatively impacted and we could experience additional cost increases.

factors.summary: The company exhibits strong pricing power, as evidenced by its ability to raise prices and improve margins despite a slight decrease in gross margin percentage. The pricing improvements, coupled with higher volumes and lower material costs, have contributed to overall gross margin expansion.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased $189 million for the three months ended July 1, 2018, versus the comparable period in 207, and decreased 1.1 points as a percentage of sales.

The increase in gross margin was primarily due to higher volumes, improved pricing and lower material costs, partially offset by $181 million for an Engine System Campaign... The increase in gross margin was primarily due to higher volumes, improved pricing, lower material costs and favorable mix.

factors.summary: Management confidence is evident across multiple areas, including increased dividends, strategic investments in new product lines and capital expenditures, and focus on research and development. The consistent actions across different financial aspects reinforce a positive outlook.

evidence.text: In July 2018, our Board of Directors authorized an increase to our quarterly dividend of 5.6 percent... Capital expenditures for the six months ended July 1, 2018, were $221 million... We plan to spend between $730 million and $760 million in 2018 on capital expenditures.

Research, development and engineering expenses increased $44 million for the three months ended July 1, 2018, versus the comparable period in 2017, primarily due to investments in the Electrified Power segment.

In July 2018, our Board of Directors authorized an increase to our quarterly dividend of 5.6 percent from $1.08 per share to $1.14 per share. During the first half of 2018, we repurchased $379 million, or 2.5 million shares of common stock.

factors.summary: The company's revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, driven by strong demand in emission solutions and power systems. Additionally, the Electrified Power segment is expected to offer significant growth opportunities due to the increasing demand for electric and hybrid powertrain solutions, with power generation markets projected to remain strong.

evidence.text: Components segment sales increased 30% primarily due to higher demand across all businesses, especially the emission solutions business, due to stronger market demand for trucks in North America and Europe. Power Systems segment sales increased 23% primarily due to higher demand for all product lines, especially in industrial markets due to higher demand in North American oil and gas markets and international mining markets.
Our Electrified Power segment will provide fully electric and hybrid powertrain solutions... We expect power generation markets to remain strong.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, primarily due to headcount growth, which is putting pressure on margins. However, productivity gains have partially offset these costs, and the situation remains manageable.

evidence.text: Increased compensation costs driven by headcount growth to support increased sales.

The increase in gross margin was primarily due to higher volumes, improved pricing and lower material costs, partially offset by $181 million for an Engine System Campaign and increased compensation costs.

factors.summary: Management has consistently provided forward guidance, reflecting confidence in future performance and expectations of continued growth in revenue and earnings.

evidence.text: Our effective tax rate for the year is expected to approximate 23.0 percent, excluding any discrete tax items that may arise.
We expect our effective tax rate for the full year of 2018 to approximate 23.0 percent, excluding any discrete tax items.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, driven by operational efficiency initiatives and cost management, with contributions from higher volumes, improved pricing, and lower material costs. However, there is a noted tension as one subsection mentions a $189 million increase in gross margin due to productivity improvements, while another highlights a $181 million decrease due to specific campaigns and increased compensation costs.

evidence.text: Productivity improvements are contributing to higher gross margins.

Gross margin increased $189 million for the three months ended July 1, 2018, versus the comparable period in 2017, and decreased 1.1 points as a percentage of sales. The increase in gross margin was primarily due to higher volumes, improved pricing, and lower material costs.

The increase in gross margin was primarily due to higher volumes, improved pricing, lower material costs and favorable mix, partially offset by $181 million for an Engine System Campaign and increased compensation costs.

factors.summary: The company faces near-term risks, including unfavorable tax items related to the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, cost increases due to trade tariffs, declining demand in China's truck market, and weak marine markets.

evidence.text: The six months ended July 1, 2018, contained $74 million, or $0.45 per share, of unfavorable net discrete tax items, primarily due to $80 million of discrete items related to the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act.

•We are experiencing cost increases as a result of trade tariffs recently imposed by the U.S. and some of its trading partners. In the event of prolonged trade disputes, demand for our products could be negatively impacted and we could experience additional cost increases. •Market demand in truck markets in China is expected to decline. •Marine markets are expected to remain weak.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is focused on innovation, electrification, and expansion into new markets, particularly through investments in the Electrified Power segment. This strategy includes developing new products to meet future emission standards and improving fuel economy performance, as well as strategic acquisitions and partnerships.

evidence.text: Research activities continue to focus on development of new products to meet future emission standards around the world and improvements in fuel economy performance of diesel and natural gas powered engines.

We invest in and utilize our internal research and development capabilities, along with strategic acquisitions and partnerships to meet our objectives. Our Electrified Power segment will provide fully electric and hybrid powertrain solutions... We are currently developing the Cummins Electric Power Battery and the Cummins Hybrid Plug-In systems for urban bus, which are expected to launch in 2019 and 2020, respectively.

factors.summary: Construction and infrastructure spending is growing, driven by strong demand in global markets, particularly in China, Europe, and North America. The company is experiencing increased unit shipments and higher demand in both construction and industrial sectors.

evidence.text: Higher demand in global construction markets, with increased international unit shipments of 39 percent primarily in China and Europe, and increased unit shipments of 10 percent in North America.
Global construction markets could remain strong... increased demand in industrial markets (especially construction and mining markets in China and Europe)

factors.summary: The company faces multiple key sensitivities including foreign currency fluctuations, interest rate changes, trade tariffs, commodity prices, and regulatory changes, which can have both favorable and unfavorable impacts on financial results. These factors may affect near-term results and require careful management.

evidence.text: Foreign currency fluctuations favorably impacted sales by 1 percent of total sales primarily in the Chinese renminbi, Euro and British pound. Interest income for the three and six months ended July 1, 2018, increased $5 million and $10 million, respectively, versus the comparable periods in 2017, primarily due to higher interest rates.

•foreign currency exchange rate changes •variability in material and commodity costs •failure to realize expected results from our investment in Eaton Cummins Automated Transmission Technologies joint venture •political, economic and other risks from operations in numerous countries •global legal and ethical compliance costs and risks •aligning our capacity and production with our demand •product liability claims •increasingly stringent environmental laws and regulations •future bans or limitations on the use of diesel-powered vehicles •the price and availability of energy •the performance of our pension plan assets and volatility of discount rates •changes in accounting standards •our sales mix of products •protection and validity of our patent and other intellectual property rights •technological implementation and cost/financial risks in our increasing use of large, multi-year contracts •the outcome of pending and future litigation and governmental proceedings •continued availability of financing, financial instruments and financial resources in the amounts, at the times and on the terms required to support our future business; and •other risk factors described in our 2017 Form 10-K, Part I, Item 1A under the caption 'Risk Factors'.
